prompt: Name a song you find genuinely moving — any language, any era — and describe, in a short paragraph, why it works on you.
response: One song that I find deeply moving is *"Ne me quitte pas"* by Jacques Brel. Sung in French, its haunting melody and raw emotional intensity capture the anguish of pleading for a lost love not to go. Brel’s passionate delivery—so full of vulnerability, desperation, and dignity—transforms the lyrics into a universal cry of the heart. The way he swings between tenderness and torment, offering everything from riches to delirium in exchange for staying, reveals both the depth of human longing and the fragility of connection. It doesn’t just tell of sorrow—it makes you feel it, deep in your bones.
